sanskrit: धूमेनाव्रियते वह्निर्यथाऽऽदर्शो मलेन च । यथोल्बेनावृतो गर्भस्तथा तेनेदमावृतम् ॥
hindiTranslation: जैसे धुएँसे अग्नि और मैलसे दर्पण ढक जाता है तथा जैसे जेरसे गर्भ ढका रहता है, ऐसे ही उस कामके द्वारा यह ज्ञान  विवेक ढका हुआ है ॥
englishTranslation: As fire is covered by smoke, a mirror by dust, and a womb by a membrane, so clear seeing is covered by desire.
essence: Clear seeing is not destroyed; it is simply covered.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Desire hides clear seeing just as smoke hides fire, dust hides a mirror, and a womb-covering hides an embryo.
simpleInsight: The problem is not lack of intelligence. Something deeper is veiling what you already know.
detailedMeaning: On Kurukshetra, Arjuna has asked why people do wrong even against their will. Krishna has named desire as the enemy, and now he shows how it works: it does not erase wisdom, it covers it, just as smoke, dust, and a womb-covering hide what is already there.
modernRelevance: You know the right thing to say, but the message sits unopened while anger blurs your judgment. The clarity is still there; something is clouding it.
translationLiteral: धूमेन (by smoke) / आव्रियते (is covered) / वह्निः (fire) / यथा (as) / आदर्शः (mirror) / मलेन (by dust) / च (and) / यथा (as) / उल्बेन (by a membrane/womb-covering) / आवृतः (covered) / गर्भः (womb/embryo) / तथा (so) / तेन (by that) / इदम् (this) / आवृतम् (is covered)
